prevalence.value: high lifetime exposure (anti-HAV IgG common); acute hepatitis A is intermittent with outbreaks
prevalence.source: Ministério da Saúde / SVSA (viral hepatitis surveillance) / Fiocruz
prevalence.trend: decreasing long-term with sanitation improvements; outbreaks occur in susceptible adults

incidence.source: Ministério da Saúde viral hepatitis notifications

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; higher in adult outbreaks
burden.mortalityRate: Very low; fulminant hepatitis is rare

riskFactors: sexual transmission in MSM networks (documented outbreaks)
vulnerablePopulations: children in areas with poor sanitation
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Acute hepatitis syndrome with anti-HAV IgM positivity; supportive LFT pattern; case definitions per Ministério da Saúde

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis in symptomatic cases and outbreak investigation
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High recovery rate; prevention via vaccination and sanitation

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: SUS lab confirmation via anti-HAV IgM; public health response during outbreaks
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care; post-exposure prophylaxis and vaccination strategies per national guidance
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Outbreaks in susceptible adult groups and sanitation disparities across municipalities
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: National immunization program and established viral hepatitis surveillance within SUS
